$comment: Single source of truth for the kun keyword vocabulary. Edit THIS file, then run: node .claude/scripts/generate-vocab.mjs — it regenerates the Vocabulary block in .claude/CLAUDE.md and src/components/docs/spellbook-data.ts. health.sh runs generate-vocab.mjs --check to flag drift. Spell fields: name (the keyword), effect (one-line), order (routing chain: familiar=agent, portal=MCP, skill=/skill-or-command, hook, ward=rule, memory), steps, connects, depends, and optional mergedInto (keyword retired into another).

schools.name: The Pipeline
schools.subtitle: Idea to production in one word
schools.description: The most powerful magic in Kun. Each keyword is a complete pipeline stage. Chain them with `feature` to go from idea to customer's hands in minutes.
schools.quote: feature billing hogwarts — one line, eight stages, live in production.

spells.name: feature
spells.effect: Chain all pipeline stages — idea to production
steps: IDEA: Create structured GitHub issue with acceptance criteria
SPEC: Data model sketch + file plan + refined criteria
SCHEMA: Prisma model + migration + Zod validation
CODE: Server actions with auth + validation + tenant isolation
WIRE: Pages + forms + tables + i18n wired to actions
WATCH: Post-deploy health check + issue closed

spells.name: idea
spells.effect: Capture a feature as a structured GitHub issue
steps: Parse feature name + optional product scope
Read product context: existing pages, models, components
Generate structured body: user story, acceptance criteria, scope

spells.name: spec
spells.effect: Technical specification — data model, file plan, human approval
steps: Survey codebase: schema, routes, components, product CLAUDE.md
Design: Prisma model sketch, file plan, refined acceptance criteria
Publish spec as comment on the issue
PAUSE: Human approves before implementation begins

spells.name: schema
spells.effect: Prisma model + migration + Zod validation schemas
steps: Create Prisma model with relations and tenant isolation
Create Zod validation schemas + TypeScript types

spells.name: ready
spells.effect: Pre-code readiness gate — validates spec, schema, and dependencies
steps: Check spec exists on issue with acceptance criteria
Check schema is migrated and types compile
Check dependencies are resolved (no blocking issues)
Check Definition of Ready checklist is met
Verdict: PASS / CONCERNS / FAIL with reasons

spells.name: code
spells.effect: Server actions with auth + Zod validation + tenant scoping
steps: Read schema and types from previous stage
Create server actions: create, list, get, update, delete
Each action: auth check → Zod validate → Prisma query → revalidate
Create authorization rules (RBAC if product uses it)

spells.name: wire
spells.effect: Pages + forms + tables + i18n — the layer users see
steps: Create form.tsx (client, React Hook Form + Zod + server actions)
Create columns.tsx (table column definitions if list view)

spells.name: check
spells.effect: Quality gate — type-check + build + visual verification
steps: Navigate to page with browser → screenshot → verify renders
Report: all gates green or list failures

spells.name: ship
spells.effect: Commit + push + deploy to Vercel production + verify
steps: Stage and commit with conventional message + Closes #N
Poll deployment status until Ready (max 10 min)
If Error: fetch logs, diagnose, fix, redeploy (max 3)

spells.name: watch
spells.effect: Post-deploy health check — confirm customer can use it
steps: Quick smoke test: click primary action, verify
If clean: close feature issue with summary

schools.name: Charm Work
schools.subtitle: Everyday practical magic
schools.description: The spells you cast most often. Reliable, essential, and dangerously easy to take for granted. Professor Flitwick would be proud.
schools.quote: dev is your Lumos — the first spell each morning. check is your Nox — the last before rest.

spells.name: dev
spells.effect: Kill port 3000, start server, open Chrome

spells.name: build
spells.effect: Compile project, scan TypeScript errors, auto-repair
steps: If errors: analyze, auto-fix, re-check (up to 5 attempts)

spells.name: push
spells.effect: Stage changes, conventional commit, push to remote

spells.name: quick
spells.effect: Fast commit and push — skip build validation

spells.name: deploy
spells.effect: Deploy to Vercel staging, retry up to 5 times
steps: Fix errors if any (loop up to 5x)
If Error: fetch logs, diagnose, fix, redeploy
If Ready: report URL and aliases

spells.name: ship
spells.effect: Deploy straight to production — no staging
steps: Same as deploy but: npx vercel --prod --yes

spells.name: check
spells.effect: Pre-ship quality gate — typecheck, build, visual, tests
steps: pnpm tsc --noEmit (type check, auto-fix loop max 5)
pnpm build (production build, auto-fix loop max 5)

spells.name: release
spells.effect: Give away to client — one spell, four stages, durable record
steps: Pre-flight: clean tree + on main + up to date
Resolve issue from --issue flag, HEAD commit, or fuzzy title match
Comment production URL + verdict on the issue; close it

schools.name: Transfiguration
schools.subtitle: Creating something from nothing
schools.description: These spells conjure new files, components, and entire features into existence. From a single component to a full feature with database, API, and UI.
schools.quote: A poorly conceived block is like a half-transfigured hedgehog — functional but unsettling. Plan before you cast.

spells.name: component
spells.effect: Conjure a new React component with proper structure
steps: Add 'use client' only if hooks needed

spells.name: page
spells.effect: Create a new Next.js App Router page

spells.name: api
spells.effect: Materialize a Server Action with validation

spells.name: atom
spells.effect: Fuse 2+ shadcn/ui primitives into a reusable component
steps: Check if atom exists in src/components/atom/
Apply design rules: single purpose, typed, className prop, RTL

spells.name: template
spells.effect: Assemble a complete page layout from established patterns
steps: Identify category (hero, header, sidebar, footer, login, dashboard)

spells.name: block
spells.effect: Full UI component with business logic, data fetching, state

spells.name: feature
spells.effect: Plan and create entire feature end-to-end

spells.name: migration
spells.effect: Create Prisma migration — reshape database safely
steps: Verify with Neon MCP if remote

schools.name: Ancient Runes
schools.subtitle: The foundational frameworks
schools.description: The ancient languages upon which everything else is built. Each carries the weight of an entire framework.
schools.quote: When you say prisma, you invoke not just a tool but a philosophy of data.

spells.name: nextjs
spells.effect: Apply Next.js 16 patterns — App Router, Server Components

spells.name: react
spells.effect: Channel React 19 — hooks, concurrent features

spells.name: typescript
spells.effect: Enforce strict mode — no any, Zod schemas, type guards

spells.name: prisma
spells.effect: Database oracle — queries, relations, Prisma 6

spells.name: tailwind
spells.effect: Tailwind CSS 4 — semantic tokens, responsive, RTL

spells.name: shadcn
spells.effect: Load the shadcn/ui knowledge pack — registry, CLI, MCP, docs-block
steps: Say 'shadcn docs' → the docs-block MDX pattern

schools.name: Conjuration
schools.subtitle: Summoning UI elements
schools.description: Each incantation calls forth a specific UI pattern — complete, styled, ready. Combine with a noun and the spell adapts.
schools.quote: table users, form settings, modal confirm — the spell adapts to your intent.

spells.name: table
spells.effect: DataTable with sorting, filtering, pagination

spells.name: header
spells.effect: Page header with navigation

spells.name: menu
spells.effect: Navigation menu or sidebar

spells.name: form
spells.effect: Form with validation

spells.name: modal
spells.effect: Dialog — appears exactly when needed

spells.name: card
spells.effect: Card variations with flexible content

spells.name: sidebar
spells.effect: Sidebar navigation — collapsible

spells.name: footer
spells.effect: Footer component — multi-column layout

spells.name: hero
spells.effect: Hero section — the grand entrance

spells.name: navbar
spells.effect: Top navigation bar

schools.name: The Dark Arts of Features
schools.subtitle: N.E.W.T.-level system enchantments
schools.description: Not dark in nature, but dark in complexity. Each creates an entire system — multiple agents, MCP servers, and spell chains working in concert.
schools.quote: saas billing orchestrates 6 familiars, opens 3 portals, and generates an entire billing system. From two words.

spells.name: auth
spells.effect: Complete authentication — NextAuth v5, JWT, OAuth, middleware

spells.name: saas
spells.effect: Database schema + server actions + UI + billing integration

spells.name: dashboard
spells.effect: Dashboard layout with charts, metrics, data viz

spells.name: landing
spells.effect: Landing page — hero, features, pricing, testimonials, footer
steps: Each section is a Server Component

spells.name: checkout
spells.effect: Payment checkout flow — cart, Stripe, confirmation

spells.name: settings
spells.effect: Settings page — forms, tabs, preferences

spells.name: profile
spells.effect: User profile — avatar, details, activity

spells.name: admin
spells.effect: Admin panel — user management, analytics

spells.name: onboarding
spells.effect: User onboarding — steps, progress, completion

schools.name: Animation Charms
schools.subtitle: Giving life to stillness
schools.description: The ancient art of making the inanimate move with purpose. Framer Motion is the wand, intent is the magic.
schools.quote: Like the Weasley twins' fireworks — magnificent in moderation, catastrophic in excess.

spells.name: motion
spells.effect: Breathe Framer Motion into components

spells.name: animation
spells.effect: CSS or Framer animations

spells.name: transition
spells.effect: Page transitions — smooth apparition between views
steps: Exit animations before new page mounts

spells.name: gesture
spells.effect: Touch and drag interactions

spells.name: scroll
spells.effect: Scroll-triggered animations

schools.name: Defense Against the Dark Arts
schools.subtitle: Protection from bugs and entropy
schools.description: The spells that protect your code from dark forces: bugs, vulnerabilities, regressions, and the slow entropy of neglect.
schools.quote: Constant vigilance! A codebase without tests is a castle without wards.

spells.name: test
spells.effect: Generate tests — Vitest for units, Playwright for journeys

spells.name: e2e
spells.effect: End-to-end tests across all browsers

spells.name: handover
spells.effect: UI verification — runs the niche keywords on a URL or every route in a block
steps: Detect mode: argument starts with / → URL mode (12 niche keywords)
Bare word → block mode (debug, flow, responsive, lang per route)
Delegate each pass to the niche keyword definition
Collect verdicts, write sentinel on PASS
Report: BLOCKED or READY FOR DEMO

spells.name: coverage
spells.effect: Reveal test coverage — the Marauder's Map

spells.name: review
spells.effect: Code review — examining decisions

spells.name: security
spells.effect: OWASP Top 10 security audit

spells.name: audit
spells.effect: Full quality audit — security + performance + quality

spells.name: analyze
spells.effect: Cross-artifact consistency — spec vs schema vs code vs tests
steps: Compare schema to spec data model
Verify code implements all spec actions

spells.name: constitution
spells.effect: Read or update project constitution — immutable governance principles

spells.name: accessibility
spells.effect: WCAG 2.1 AA compliance check

spells.name: optimize
spells.effect: Performance optimization

spells.name: performance
spells.effect: Core Web Vitals diagnostic

schools.name: Reparo
schools.subtitle: The mending spells
schools.description: When things break — and they will — these incantations diagnose and repair with surgical precision.
schools.quote: A true master does not fear errors. They cast fix and move on.

spells.name: fix
spells.effect: Auto-repair all lint, type, and build errors

spells.name: error
spells.effect: Scan for errors and fix them
steps: Missing schoolId, hooks in server components

spells.name: scan
spells.effect: Sweep entire codebase for issues

spells.name: lint
spells.effect: Run ESLint
steps: Report issues with file paths and lines

spells.name: format
spells.effect: Run Prettier
steps: Auto-Format hook already runs on every edit

spells.name: type-check
spells.effect: TypeScript strict checking — Veritaserum for types

spells.name: report
spells.effect: Auto-fix user-reported issues — read, verify, fix, close
steps: Session start: check open report issues across all repos
READ: gh issue view → extract page URL + description
LOCATE: URL → route directory (src/app/) + component directory (src/components/)
CONTEXT: read CLAUDE.md, README.md, ISSUE.md for the feature
VALIDATE: real bug? aligned with plans? safe to fix?
SEE + DEBUG: screenshot page, check console errors, network failures
IDENTIFY: correlate report + visual + errors → root cause
FIX: edit minimum code in component directory
BUILD: pnpm build — verify no regressions
PUSH: conventional commit (fix: ...) + push to main
VERIFY: see the page again after deploy
CLOSE: close issue with fix summary

schools.name: Quill Charms
schools.subtitle: The spells of documentation
schools.description: Often neglected, always essential. Like Madam Pince guarding the library, these preserve knowledge.
schools.quote: The Memory Charms done right — instead of erasing knowledge, they preserve it.

spells.name: docs
spells.effect: Generate MDX documentation

spells.name: readme
spells.effect: Create or update the README

spells.name: api-docs
spells.effect: API documentation — every endpoint

spells.name: storybook
spells.effect: Component stories

spells.name: changelog
spells.effect: Update changelog from commit history

schools.name: Geminio
schools.subtitle: Duplication and summoning
schools.description: Why build from scratch? These clone, copy, and adapt existing work — the Geminio charm applied to code.
schools.quote: clone table from codebase summons the DataTable. clone vercel/ai reaches across GitHub.

spells.name: clone
spells.effect: Clone from codebase or any GitHub repo

spells.name: copy
spells.effect: Copy a component — identical but independent
steps: Same as clone, source always local

spells.name: fork
spells.effect: Create a variant — free to diverge

spells.name: extend
spells.effect: Inherit and modify — building on giants

spells.name: sync
spells.effect: Synchronize with upstream source

spells.name: upstream
spells.effect: Pull changes from upstream

schools.name: Summoning Charms
schools.subtitle: Portals to external realms
schools.description: Each word opens a portal to a powerful service beyond your machine. The Floo Network, but for code.
schools.quote: Each portal stays open for the duration of your session. Use them freely.

spells.name: github
spells.effect: Repos, PRs, issues, Actions, code search

spells.name: figma
spells.effect: Design files, components, visual truth

spells.name: linear
spells.effect: Issues, projects, sprint tracking

spells.name: slack
spells.effect: Messages, channels, team communication

spells.name: notion
spells.effect: Documents, databases, wikis

spells.name: sentry
spells.effect: Error monitoring, exception traces

spells.name: stripe
spells.effect: Payments, subscriptions, invoices

spells.name: vercel
spells.effect: Deployments, domains, the edge

spells.name: analytics
spells.effect: User behavior, funnels, feature flags

spells.name: neon
spells.effect: PostgreSQL branches, database management

schools.name: Divination
schools.subtitle: Seeing ahead
schools.description: Planning, architecting, predicting what must be built. Not all wizards believe, but the best ones practice daily.
schools.quote: Trelawney was wrong about most things, but right about one: prepare for the future.

spells.name: bmad
spells.effect: Show BMAD menu — choose planning track

spells.name: flow
spells.effect: Quick Flow — five minutes to a working plan

spells.name: plan
spells.effect: Enter planning phase — architecture first

spells.name: architect
spells.effect: Design system architecture

spells.name: implement
spells.effect: Execute the plan

spells.name: story
spells.effect: Create user stories

spells.name: cycle
spells.effect: Full development cycle

spells.name: loop
spells.effect: Continuous iteration — never ends

schools.name: Advanced Spellwork
schools.subtitle: Performance magic
schools.description: N.E.W.T.-level incantations. They don't create — they refine. Faster, smaller, sharper.
schools.quote: parallelize alone can improve performance 2-10x. This is not theory — it is power.

spells.name: parallelize
spells.effect: Sequential to Promise.all() — the Time-Turner

spells.name: waterfall
spells.effect: Detect and eliminate request waterfalls

spells.name: bundle
spells.effect: Analyze bundle, recommend splits — Reducio

spells.name: lazy
spells.effect: Dynamic imports — appear only when summoned

spells.name: suspense
spells.effect: Suspense boundaries — progressive loading

spells.name: memo
spells.effect: useMemo/useCallback — preserve computations

spells.name: server-component
spells.effect: Convert to Server Component
steps: Extract hooks into child Client Components

spells.name: streaming
spells.effect: Streaming with Suspense — progressive results

spells.name: barrel
spells.effect: Fix barrel imports — eliminate 200-800ms curse

spells.name: dedup
spells.effect: React.cache() — the spell that remembers

schools.name: Portkeys
schools.subtitle: Teleportation to other repos
schools.description: Speak a reference incantation and your context shifts to another repository — its patterns, its hard-won wisdom.
schools.quote: auth like hogwarts transports the auth pattern. table from codebase summons the DataTable.

spells.name: from codebase
spells.effect: The pattern library — 54 primitives, 62 atoms, 31 templates

spells.name: from shadcn
spells.effect: shadcn/ui fork — refined for Databayt

spells.name: from radix
spells.effect: Radix primitives — the raw building blocks

spells.name: like hogwarts
spells.effect: Education SaaS — multi-tenant, LMS, billing

spells.name: like souq
spells.effect: Marketplace — vendors, carts, products

spells.name: like mkan
spells.effect: Rental platform — listings, bookings, search

spells.name: like shifa
spells.effect: Medical system — appointments, patient records

spells.name: like sijillee
spells.effect: Sijillee — repo pending; context lives in the CRM workspace + catalog only
steps: Read the sijillee entry in memory/repositories.json
No GitHub repo yet — skip clone and code search
Pull product context from the CRM workspace + catalog
Borrow the nearest patterns from hogwarts until the repo lands

spells.name: like moallimee
spells.effect: Moallimee — repo pending; context lives in the CRM workspace + catalog only
steps: Read the moallimee entry in memory/repositories.json
No GitHub repo yet — skip clone and code search
Pull product context from the CRM workspace + catalog
Borrow the nearest patterns from hogwarts until the repo lands

schools.name: The Unforgivable Commands
schools.subtitle: Forbidden by the engine
schools.description: Denied by settings.json. No agent, skill, or charm can override them. Some magic cannot be undone.
schools.quote: The Ministry had its reasons. So does Kun.

spells.name: rm -rf *
spells.effect: Catastrophic deletion — the Avada Kedavra of filesystems
steps: Blocked by deny rule in settings.json

spells.name: prisma migrate reset
spells.effect: Wipes entire database — every table, every record

spells.name: prisma db push --accept-data-loss
spells.effect: Accepts data loss

spells.name: DROP TABLE
spells.effect: Destroy table via Neon MCP

spells.name: git push --force main
spells.effect: Overwrite shared history

schools.name: The Auror Office
schools.subtitle: One keyword, one quality dimension
schools.description: The niche inspectors. Each spell checks exactly one dimension of quality — six through the browser, six through the code, two deep, two comparative. The quality familiar routes them all; handover casts them together.
schools.quote: handover /admission/new — twelve inspections, one verdict table.

spells.name: see
spells.effect: Visual check — page loads, layout holds, content renders
steps: Navigate to the URL and screenshot
Verify the page loads — no blank or broken sections
Check layout, spacing, and content render as intended
Verdict: PASS / WARN / FAIL with what to fix

spells.name: flow
spells.effect: Interactive check — click, type, submit through the user journey
steps: Walk the journey — click, type, submit
Verify navigation and form submissions land
Confirm success and error states surface
Verdict names the exact step that broke

spells.name: debug
spells.effect: Error check — console, network, uncaught exceptions
steps: Open the route via the browser MCP
Report zero-error PASS or list each exception with its source

spells.name: responsive
spells.effect: Layout check at mobile 375, tablet 768, desktop 1440
steps: Check overflow, wrapping, and contested space

spells.name: lang
spells.effect: Language check — RTL, LTR, translation completeness
steps: Load the route in both ar and en locales
Check RTL mirroring — direction, logical properties
Scan for hardcoded strings missing from dictionaries
List every untranslated string in the verdict

spells.name: fast
spells.effect: Speed check — quick Core Web Vitals health
steps: Load the page and sample Core Web Vitals
Check LCP, CLS, INP against budget
Flag oversized payloads and blocking requests
PASS, or hand the deep work to trace

spells.name: guard
spells.effect: Security check — auth, validation, tenant scope
steps: Read the route's layout, actions, and queries
Verify auth guard at the boundary + per-action authz
Verify Zod validation and tenant scoping on every query
Check S3 access uses presigned URLs via CloudFront

spells.name: architecture
spells.effect: Architecture check — mirror pattern, boundaries, data flow
steps: Trace the route's data flow from page to query
Verify mirror pattern and server/client boundaries

spells.name: structure
spells.effect: File check — naming, directory, placement
steps: Map the block's files against the mirror-pattern layout
Check naming conventions and directory placement
Flag each misplaced or misnamed file with its correct location

spells.name: pattern
spells.effect: Convention check — page, actions, form against the canonical cards
steps: Compare page, actions, and form to the canonical triplet

spells.name: design
spells.effect: Component check — ui/atom/template hierarchy and token discipline
steps: Verify tokens — OKLCH colors, no hardcoded values

spells.name: stack
spells.effect: Technology check — versions, imports, deprecated APIs across every rule domain
steps: Scan imports for deprecated APIs and wrong versions
Check React 19 / Next 16 / Prisma 6 / Tailwind 4 idioms

spells.name: trace
spells.effect: Deep performance investigation — find the bottleneck and fix it
steps: Record a performance trace on the slow route
Isolate the bottleneck — query, waterfall, bundle, or render

spells.name: efficient
spells.effect: Code efficiency — cut duplicate work and reduce API calls
steps: Count API calls and queries per interaction
Propose the reduction — dedup, batch, select, cache

spells.name: mirror
spells.effect: Compare Figma design against the live implementation
steps: Pull the Figma frame — screenshot + design context
Screenshot the implementation at the same size
Diff spacing, type, color, and structure
Report each divergence — a human judges intent

spells.name: diff
spells.effect: Compare URL against URL — visual divergence report
steps: Screenshot both URLs at the same breakpoints
Diff layout, spacing, color, and copy
Report each divergence side by side

schools.name: The Ministry of Magic
schools.subtitle: Operations and intelligence
schools.description: The spells that keep the company running and learning — weekly cadence, production watch, incident response, credential vaults, and the intelligence that extracts the org's own patterns from its history.
schools.quote: monitor watches production while weekly plans the week — the Ministry never sleeps.

spells.name: weekly
spells.effect: The captain's cadence — Monday plan, Wednesday check, Friday review
steps: Monday: allocate the week — people, priorities, value-at-stake
Wednesday: check progress against the plan
Friday: review, coach, rotate a canon principle

spells.name: monitor
spells.effect: Deployments, costs, and uptime across every databayt product
steps: Check Vercel deployment status for all products
Sum API + infra spend against budget

spells.name: incident
spells.effect: Production incident response — classify, diagnose, fix, postmortem
steps: Classify severity — P0 drop-everything to P3
Pull Sentry traces + Vercel logs to diagnose
Fix or roll back, verify recovery
Write the postmortem on a GitHub issue

spells.name: credentials
spells.effect: Databayt credentials — API keys via Keychain, web logins via Safari
steps: status: show which keys and passwords are configured
setup: store a new API key in the Keychain
login: drive Safari autofill for web services
export: surface a key for env wiring

spells.name: health
spells.effect: Config health across the team + engine drift check
steps: Run health.sh — MCP, hooks, skills, agents
Diff reality against engine.json counts and model refs
Warn on doc rot — drift is warn-only, never a gate

spells.name: learn
spells.effect: Extract organizational patterns and conventions from repos + history
steps: Scan git history across the databayt repos
Extract patterns, conventions, and team dynamics
Write findings to memory for reuse
Feed the conventions, patterns, and drift surfaces

spells.name: conventions
spells.effect: Alias of learn — surface the org's coding conventions on demand
steps: Filter for naming, structure, and style conventions
Answer with examples from real repos

spells.name: patterns
spells.effect: Alias of learn — list the recurring patterns across the org's repos
steps: Rank patterns by adoption across repos
Point to the canonical card when one exists

spells.name: drift
spells.effect: Alias of the learn/health surface — spot config and docs drifting from reality
steps: Diff engine.json against repo reality and docs
List each drifted count, model ref, or stale doc

schools.name: The Pensieve
schools.subtitle: Judgment and conversion
schools.description: Dip into stored wisdom. canon pulls the right CEO book into a live decision — passively, mid-conversation; convert distills any file or URL into Markdown the engine can read.
schools.quote: "should we build it?" — the Pensieve surfaces The Lean Startup before you finish the sentence.

spells.name: canon
spells.effect: Surface the right book + one operating move for a business or leadership decision — passive, no slash needed
steps: Detect the decision type in natural conversation — hiring, pricing, positioning, runway, build-or-not
Map it via the retrieval index to one of the 10 books
Surface one book + one operating move, grounded in principle #N
Keep it brief; skip trivial or purely technical choices

spells.name: convert
spells.effect: File or URL to Markdown — PDF, Office, images, audio, web
steps: Detect the source — local file or URL
Return clean Markdown ready for docs or memory

spells.name: higgs
spells.effect: Generate and edit photos and videos for databayt org ads/marketing via the Higgsfield CLI — zero-question recipes, brand kit pre-wired, batch-first
steps: Preflight: account status; self-heal auth + workspace if needed
Pick the recipe + model from the skill's verified defaults (never ask)
Estimate free (generate cost / --cost-only / --enhance-only), then batch-generate with --json
Parse .[].result_url, download to ~/Downloads/higgs/, deliver via SendUserFile with spend + balance

workflows.name: Feature Pipeline
workflows.description: The full pipeline — idea to customer's hands

steps.action: Structured GitHub issue with acceptance criteria
Data model + file plan + human approval
Server actions with auth + tenant isolation
